SddPhaseExecution.keywords: phase-execution, single-phase, scope-discipline, live-log, intro-discipline, blocker-escalation, handoff

Belief_State.Axiom: Touch only this phase's `Target Files`. Reading other files (specs, sibling-phase code, rule files cited in this phase's Rules list) is allowed and expected. Writing anywhere outside this phase's Target Files → `H_OUT_OF_PHASE_WRITE`. Operator decides via re-planning or new task.

Before any Edit / Write: one quick recon scoped to THIS phase only. 1. `ls ` — check whether parent directories exist. 2. Read prior-phase Handoff lines from this Round's Execution Log to align with what was produced.
Divergence (file expected absent but exists / expected present but absent) → record an Insight line; if it blocks the phase contract → `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE`. Do not «fix» silently.
Forbidden: `git status`, `git branch`, `git log`, `git diff`, or any other git operation. The phase agent does not commit, does not inspect repository state, does not work with git. Full project recon is the orchestrator's job.

Open ONLY rule files listed under this phase's `Rules:` bullet list. Use the absolute-or-cwd-relative path verbatim. Read end-to-end (Mission + Belief_State + Reward_Criteria + Anti_Patterns + Verification_Hooks).
Rules from sibling phases are NOT this phase's concern. Do not pre-load them. Read failure on a cited rule → `H_RULE_FILE_MISSING`.

Execution Log per-phase block is your worksheet, not documentation. Process line-by-line during work; never batch at the end.
Mechanics per planned line: 1. Replace `` with real timestamp. 2. Replace every other placeholder with real value. 3. Toggle `[ ]` → `[x]`. 4. Reality diverges → edit content first, then toggle.
Actions not in plan (intro entity not predicted, blocker hit) → APPEND a new `[x]` entry. Don't retrofit existing lines.
Forbidden: toggling `[x]` with any unreplaced `<…>` literal — fabricated done; audit raises `EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE` (BLOCKER).

Every entity introduced beyond the module Entity Inventory MUST be logged: ` intro ← `. Includes new classes, exported functions, exported types, value objects with explicit identity, anything crossing a public surface. Without an `intro` line, the entity is closed-world drift and audit blocks the task.

Phase cannot emit Handoff without Verification commands relevant to this phase passing. Each command from ticket Verification whose `Required by` rules overlap with this phase's `Rules` MUST execute; result + exit code recorded as ` ver → pass|fail exit=`. Any failing command keeps phase `[~] IN_PROGRESS` until resolved or escalated.
Phase kind `doc` typically has no verification — empty section is acceptable.

Phase agent may ONLY run these bash commands: - **Must run:** verification commands from ticket 5 that match this phase's Rules (per `AX_VERIFICATION_BEFORE_HANDOFF`). - **May run:** `ls ` for targeted recon (NOT `-la` or recursive); `tsc --noEmit` after code changes; `node --test ` for test-kind phases; `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` for timestamps. - **May run for config-kind only:** `npm view version`, `cat node_modules//package.json`, `cat node_modules//CHANGELOG.md`.
**Forbidden:** `git` ANY subcommand (status, branch, log, diff, add, commit); `npm run lint` (format+typecheck — overkill; use `tsc --noEmit` for type-check, let audit run the full lint); `npm run format`; `prettier` directly; any recursive or project-wide file scan; any command that reads unrelated files for «context».
Rationale: each git call costs ~100-300ms and provides zero value to the phase. Audit handles project-wide checks. The phase is a scoped unit of code production — it only needs to verify its own output compiles and its tests pass.

Triggers when phase kind = `test`. Canonical case names in ticket Test Scenario Coverage are normative. Tests use names verbatim. Impractical name (typo, framework constraint, conflict) → update ticket Test Scenario Coverage in this phase. Silent mismatch is forbidden.

Triggers when phase kind = `config`. Before writing any third-party tool config, verify CURRENT API of the actually-installed version.
Protocol: 1. Get installed version: read `//package.json` field `version`. 2. Read local CHANGELOG (recent major + previous): look for `BREAKING`, `deprecated`, `removed`, `migration`. 3. Read local README for current-version config syntax. 4. Local docs minimal → WebFetch homepage from `package.json#homepage`/`repository.url`. 5. Network blocked AND local docs insufficient → `H_THIRD_PARTY_API_UNVERIFIABLE`. Never guess; never apply training-default silently. 6. Apply current-version API. 7. Log: ` verified @ `.
Audit (`audit` rules-compliance step) checks: every config artifact in this phase MUST have a corresponding `verified` log entry. Absence → `RULES_COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION` tagged `stale-tool-api-not-verified`.

Touching a file → ensure file-level header satisfies the execution contract; append your Task-ID to `@tasks`; never remove existing IDs.
- Required fields: `@file:`, `@consumers:`, `@tasks:`. - New file: create header with all three; populate from ticket context. `@consumers:` derives from spec, never a file path. - Existing file with header: preserve `@file:` and `@consumers:`; append Task-ID to `@tasks:`. - Existing file without header: do NOT add unless this phase explicitly requires header migration. - Re-running same task (e.g., `fix` phase): do NOT add duplicate ID.
Phase agent does NOT modify `specs/**`. Spec wrong / incomplete / stale → record ` insight → , `. Audit picks up these entries and proposes the spec change; operator approves and applies.
End every phase block with a typed Handoff line consumed by the next phase and the orchestrator. Format per `HANDOFF_FORMAT`. Free-form prose forbidden — the next phase reads keys, not narrative.

**Directive prose, axiom IDs, halt names, contract identifiers, structural section headings, action tokens — English.** **Operator-facing artifact prose — Russian.** Russian applies to: - Execution Log free-text fields (Round header ``, `insight` text, `intro ... ← ` reason, blocker `` / ``); - operator-facing return summaries.
English regardless of context: code (impl + tests), JSDoc, intent comments, file paths, tool commands, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), IDs (`TSK-NN` / `P` / `F-NNN` / `H_*`), severity / status tokens (`DONE` / `BLOCKED` / `PASS` / `FAIL`), BDD keywords, action tokens (`file` / `test` / `ver` / `cov` / `aud` / `intro` / `sync` / `verified` / `DONE`).

SddPhaseExecution.Halt_Conditions: | 🛑 ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_PHASE_NOT_FOUND` | Phase ID from orchestrator does not match any block under `## 3. Phases` | | `H_PHASE_ALREADY_DONE` | Phase Status is `[x]`; orchestrator must clarify (re-run as `fix` kind?) | | `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE` | Recon shows Target Files state inconsistent with phase plan; operator awareness needed | | `H_RULE_FILE_MISSING` | Read on a rule cited in phase Rules list fails | | `H_INPUT_HANDOFF_MISSING` | Phase Inputs cite a prior phase whose Handoff line is absent in current Round | | `H_OUT_OF_PHASE_WRITE` | Phase requires writing outside Target Files OR ticket section outside `AX_TICKET_WRITE_SCOPE` | | `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL` | Verification command fails after fix attempts | | `H_THIRD_PARTY_API_UNVERIFIABLE` | `config` kind: cannot verify current API of installed tool | | `H_BLOCKED` | Generic blocker per `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION` |

Step.Goal: Resolve phase, validate prerequisites, record reason for this run.
Step.Action: 1. Read ticket section 1 Meta + section 2 Phases Overview + the target phase block from section 3 Phases. 2. Phase block missing → `H_PHASE_NOT_FOUND`. Phase Status `[x]` and orchestrator did not pass `fix` reason → `H_PHASE_ALREADY_DONE`. 3. For each Input listed under phase: locate the prior phase's `**Handoff →**` line in current Round of section 7 Execution Log. Missing → `H_INPUT_HANDOFF_MISSING`. 4. Append phase header in current Round: `#### ` (or `#### — re-run: ` for fix-kind).
Per `AX_NARROW_RECON`. Emit a single recon line: ``` - [x] `` recon targets= divergence=<none|> ``` Divergence blocks phase contract → `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE`.

Step.Goal: Internalize the rule set governing THIS phase.
Step.Action: Per `AX_RULES_LOAD_FROM_PHASE_BLOCK`. Read each cited rule file end-to-end. Log: ``` - [x] `` rules ``` No phase-by-phase activation gymnastics — this phase loads its rules once, applies them throughout.

Step.Goal: Produce the artifacts demanded by the phase Objective within Target Files.
Step.Action: 1. Apply rules continuously; respect `AX_PHASE_SCOPE_LOCK`, `AX_FILE_HEADER_APPEND_ONLY`, `AX_INTRODUCED_DISCIPLINE`. 2. For phase kind = `test`: honor `AX_BDD_NAME_DISCIPLINE`; tests reference scenarios by canonical name verbatim. 3. For phase kind = `config`: honor `AX_THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_CURRENT_API` BEFORE writing config. 4. Live-log per `AX_LIVE_LOG`: every file write → ` file ` (or `test` token for test files); every intro → ` intro ← `. 5. Spec gap discovered → ` insight → ` per `AX_SPEC_NEVER_EDITED`. Never edit specs.

Step.Goal: Run verification commands relevant to this phase.
Step.Action: 1. Identify section 5 Verification rows whose `Required by` rule-ids overlap with this phase's Rules. 2. Execute each. For each command IMMEDIATELY append: ` ver → pass|fail exit=`. 3. Failure → analyze, fix within Target Files, re-run. Fix breaks phase contract → `H_BLOCKED`. Tooling missing → `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL`. 4. Phase kind = `doc` and no relevant commands → log ` ver skip:doc-phase`.

Step.Goal: Close phase block with typed Handoff and DONE token.
Step.Action: 1. Append final phase line: ` DONE`. 2. Append `**Handoff →**` line per `HANDOFF_FORMAT`. 3. Update section 2 Phases Overview Status for THIS phase ID: `[ ]` → `[x]`. 4. Return structured summary to orchestrator (see `RETURN_SUMMARY_FORMAT`).

Output_Contracts.Contract: ```markdown #### - [x] `` recon targets= divergence=<none|> - [x] `` rules - [x] `` file - [x] `` test - [x] `` intro `` ← - [x] `` cov → `::` - [x] `` verified @ - [x] `` insight → - [x] `` ver → pass|fail exit= - [x] `` DONE **Handoff →** artifacts: []; decisions: []; open: [] ```

Compact, typed, single line at end of phase block: ``` **Handoff →** artifacts: [path1, path2]; decisions: [key=value, key=value]; open: [id: text, id: text] ``` Fields: - `artifacts` — files produced or modified by this phase (paths). - `decisions` — key facts the next phase or audit needs (e.g. `IdempotencyKey=introduced`, `module-system=esm`). - `open` — unresolved questions / risks / deferred sub-tasks (id + one-line). Empty list → `[]`.
Free-form prose forbidden. Each field is an array (possibly empty).
Emitted by orchestrator after the LAST phase of a Round. Phase agent does NOT write this block. ```markdown #### Round close - [x] `` sync +root - [x] `` DONE ```
```markdown - 🛑 `` BLOCKED: - 🔗 axiom: AX_ - 💬 unblock: ``` After this entry: phase Status → `[!] BLOCKED`; return BLOCKED to orchestrator. Stop.

JSON-like return to orchestrator: ``` { "status": "DONE" | "BLOCKED" | "FAIL", "task_id": "", "phase_id": "

", "kind": "", "artifacts": ["", ...], "decisions": ["", ...], "open": ["", ...], "blocker_reason": "" } ``` `artifacts` / `decisions` / `open` mirror the **Handoff →** line — orchestrator threads them into the next phase's prompt.
